rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Motion For Summary Judgment Hearing Date
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday December 14, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, VLAD COOD Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Motion For Summary Judgment Hearing Date GRANTED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of all relevant briefing no later than December 21, 2018 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Plaintiff to file and sever notice of hearing for January 9, 2019 no later than December 17, 2018. Proof of Service to be filed no later than December 18, 2018. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD MIRANDA VS. 1883 14TH AVENUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment In Conformance With The Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday December 14, 2018 line 2. PLAINTIFF EDWARD MIRANDA, ELAINE MIRANDA Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment In Conformance With The Award GRANTED. Arbitration Award is confirmed. Request for fees is GRANTED: (1) Arbitration properly conducted per parties' contractual agreement and Court's Order issued pursuant to Defendant's motion. Defendant's attempt to attack judge Bradstreet's Order is improper; (2) No prejudicial error committed by the arbitrator by disallowing expert testimony re: measure of damages. Measure of damages is a question of law; (3) The measure of damages is no a proper subject of this Court's review. Even if the Court could review this issue, the choice of measure of damages resulted in more favorable outcome for the defendant, i.e. if the Court were to change the measure of damages to diminution of value, the award against the Defendant would increase by $253,000. The total amount to be recovered from Defendants is $1,664,047.01 per arbitration award, including attorney's fees. = (501/REQ)

Service by mail on November 19, 2018 did not provide 5 additional days for mailing. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday December 14, 2018 line 4. PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike To First Amended Complaint DENIED. Per CRC 3.1110(a) "A notice of motion must state in the opening paragraph the nature of the order being sought and the grounds for issuance of the order." The only stated grounds for the Motion to Strike in the opening paragraph of the Notice of Motion to Strike is "irrelevant." (Notice of Motion 1:27). All other purported grounds for the Motion are not properly before the Court as they are not properly noticed. In the Points and Authorities and in the Motion the "irrelevant" paragraphs are identified as 84, 99, 121, 166, 177, and 189. No authority is cited addressing irrelevancy of each particular paragraph. No argument is provided as to each particular paragraph. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN C. HO VS. RS LENDING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday December 14, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT RS LENDING, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action GRANTED. Lis Pendens recorded on July 3, 2018 is expunged. Plaintiff failed to comply with proper procedure. Plaintiff failed to cite any authority for the proposition that procedural defects in the Lis Pendens may be cured by filing a new Lis Pendens while a motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is pending. Additionally, Plaintiff failed to establish probable validity of his claim irrespective of procedural defects. = (501/REQ)
